subject: Project Management
title: Military Procurement Nightmares
description: A fast breakdown of Military Procurement Nightmares for builders.

hook.text: The F-35 fighter jet program is estimated to cost $1.7 trillion over its lifetime. It's the most expensive weapon ever.

buildup.text: Originally budgeted at $233 billion, costs spiraled as one aircraft tried to serve three military branches.

discovery.text: Software alone accounts for over 8 million lines of code — more than any previous fighter in history.

twist.text: The military consistently low-balls initial estimates to get political approval, then adjusts upward later.

climax.text: Military procurement shows the planning fallacy at its largest scale — with taxpayers footing the bill.

punchline.text: Promise cheap. Deliver expensive. Repeat forever.

quiz.question: What is the estimated lifetime cost of the F-35 program?
